text: Writing clean code is an essential skill for any programmer. The goal is to make your code as readable as possible, so that you and other developers can understand it easily. One important practice is using clear naming conventions. Variable and function names should describe exactly what they do, like `calculateTotalPrice` instead of `calc`. Additionally, it's helpful to break your code into small, focused functions. Each function should do just one thing well, making the code simpler to test. Clean code is much easier to maintain over time, which means fewer bugs and faster updates for your projects.
